case_id: Oswego County, NY - April 18, 1940 Church Supper

pi_items.source: CDC Epi Info 7 documentation and 2x2 table analysis: 43 persons exposed to vanilla ice cream who became ill
CDC Epi Info 7 documentation and 2x2 table analysis: 11 persons exposed to vanilla ice cream who did not become ill
CDC Epi Info 7 documentation and 2x2 table analysis: 3 persons not exposed to vanilla ice cream who became ill
CDC Epi Info 7 documentation and 2x2 table analysis: 18 persons not exposed to vanilla ice cream who did not become ill
Standard epidemiological tolerance for diagnostic metrics. All calculated metrics fall within ±0.05 of the metadata values.

pi_items.metadata_value: Oswego outbreak project/dataset via Epi Info 7
pi_items.verified_value: Real historical CDC epidemiological dataset from Oswego County, NY outbreak of April 18, 1940
pi_items.source: CDC Epidemic Intelligence Service (EIS) case study materials, https://www.cdc.gov/eis/downloads/xoswego-401-303-student.pdf; also available in R packages epitools and epiDisplay

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real epidemiological data from the Oswego County, New York gastrointestinal illness outbreak of April 18, 1940. Of 75 persons who attended a church supper, 46 became ill. A 2x2 contingency table for vanilla ice cream exposure vs. illness shows: 43 exposed and ill, 11 exposed but not ill, 3 not exposed but ill, 18 not exposed and not ill. All ground truth metrics are verified: Sensitivity=0.93 (43/46), Specificity=0.62 (18/29), PPV=0.80 (43/54), NPV=0.86 (18/21). This is a real historical dataset maintained by CDC and widely used for epidemiology training since the 1950s.
